_README: LLM-derived 0–3 axis scores for amari (axes: bitter, sweet, citrus, herbal, dark, mint, root). Bitter is the defining axis; sweet opposes; the rest distinguish families (Fernet = mint, Cynar = root, Averna = dark, Aperol/Campari = citrus, Suze = bitter+root). Classics scored from canonical knowledge; artisanals scored from BA descriptions + producer notes. Smoky amari (Sfumato, Zucca) map smoke to 'dark'. Re-score after personal tasting.
_design_notes: The 'root' axis captures earthy/vegetal/rhizome character (gentian, rhubarb, walnut, artichoke, chinotto). The 'dark' axis captures coffee/chocolate/molasses/cola/smoke — burnt or brown registers. They're independent: Cynar is root-high dark-low; Averna is dark-high root-low; Sfumato is both.

341.name: Aperol
341.reasoning: Canonical Italian aperitivo. Bittersweet orange, low bitter intensity, gentle herbal. Highest sweet+citrus, lowest bitter of the aperitivo family.

174.name: Campari
174.reasoning: Iconic Italian aperitivo. Bitter orange + chinotto + cinchona. Mid-bitter, sweetened to balance. Defines the bittersweet-aperitivo family.

131.name: Fernet-Branca
131.reasoning: Canonical Italian fernet. Maximum bitter, zero sweet, dominant menthol/peppermint. The Fernet family archetype: bitter + mint + dry.

249.name: Fernet Paesani
249.reasoning: Italian fernet, lower-sugar than typical amari but slightly less aggressive than Fernet-Branca. Same family character; menthol present but less peppermint-piercing.

592.name: Fred Jerbis Fernet 25 Chestnut Barrel
592.reasoning: Friuli small-producer fernet: 25 botanicals (saffron, licorice, mint, myrrh, chamomile, pine, rhubarb, gentian), chestnut-barrel aged 40-60 days. Producer notes describe it as 'drier, lighter, more floral than Fernet-Branca' — so less aggressive bitter/mint, more herbal-floral character.

130.name: Cynar Ricetta Originale
130.reasoning: Carciofo (artichoke) amaro. Vegetal/earthy root character is defining. Moderate bitter, modest sweet, dark notes from caramelization.

509.name: Cynar 70
509.reasoning: Higher-proof (70 proof / 35% ABV) version of Cynar Ricetta — same recipe and character, just more concentrated. Body axis isn't in the amaro set but the profile vector is unchanged.

190.name: Amaro Averna
190.reasoning: Sicilian dark Italian digestivo. Cola/molasses/citrus peel. Defines the dark/cola family. Mid-bitter, mid-sweet, max dark.

127.name: Amaro Montenegro
127.reasoning: Sweet/floral Italian digestivo. Orange + vanilla + violet + herbal complexity. Lowest bitter, highest sweet of the classical digestivi.

128.name: Amaro Nonino Quintessentia
128.reasoning: Friulian alpine amaro on a grappa base (gives it distinctive lighter, drier body). Mountain herbs dominate; lower sweet than Italian classics.

529.name: Amaro Ramazzotti
529.reasoning: Dark Italian digestivo (Milan). Earthier and more herbal than Averna; still dark/sweet-forward. Anise notes give it slight registered complexity.

537.name: Amaro Meletti
537.reasoning: Marche-region amaro with saffron and chocolate notes. Floral-forward dark Italian, lighter than Averna. Distinctive anise/saffron register.

486.name: Amaro Braulio
486.reasoning: Valtellina alpine amaro. Mountain herbs (yarrow, gentian, juniper berries), wood-aged. Drier than Nonino; subtle mint from alpine herbs.

609.name: Amaro CioCiaro
609.reasoning: Bitter-orange-forward Italian amaro. Canonical Amer Picon substitute (Brooklyn, Pausa's Bastardo). Drier than Averna with pronounced citrus + gentian.

523.name: Amaro Abano
523.reasoning: Veneto amaro, medium-bodied with herbal and slightly minty notes. Used in Mott and Mulberry. Mid-bitter, modest sweet, herbal-forward with a touch of mint.

187.name: Suze Saveur d'Autrefois
187.reasoning: French pure-gentian aperitif. Maximum bitter, maximum root (gentian IS the root axis), low sweet. The benchmark for gentian-forward bitter.

543.name: Bonal Gentiane-Quina
543.reasoning: French quinquina-style aperitif: wine base + gentian + cinchona. Lighter and sweeter than Suze; structurally closer to a sweet vermouth than a spirit-based amaro.

170.name: Becherovka Original
170.reasoning: Czech herbal liqueur (1807). Anise + cinnamon-forward, sweet, low bitter. Herbal character with subtle mint. Distinctive among amari for its baking-spice profile.

604.name: Cappelletti Pasubio Vino Amaro
604.reasoning: Dolomite wine-based amaro (17% ABV — structurally closer to vermouth). Wild blueberries (mirtilli) smoked over pine, plus alpine herbs. Distinctive smoke-fruit profile.

612.name: Cappelletti Sfumato Rabarbaro
612.reasoning: Smoky rhubarb amaro. Defining note is smoke-over-rhubarb (rhubarb root dried via smoking process). Roasted/tea-like/tarry/earthy/vegetal. Smoke character maps to 'dark' axis here; rhubarb root pushes 'root' to max.

608.name: Bordiga Centum Herbis
608.reasoning: Piedmontese amaro with 100 botanicals. Alpine tradition; layered, complex herbal profile. Terroir-driven mountain character.

487.name: Brucato Amaro Chaparral
487.reasoning: California amaro with Sierra Nevada wild botanicals: sagebrush, yerba santa, manzanita. Distinctly Western herbal — pine + sage character.

129.name: Brucato Orchards
129.reasoning: Arizona-style amaro: apricot, walnut, Meyer lemon, orange, cinnamon. Fruit-forward and citrus-bright; walnut adds root/earthy bridge. Low bitter, high sweet.

248.name: Faccia Brutto Amaro Gorini
248.reasoning: Brooklyn solera-aged amaro: 13 botanicals, whiskey-barrel aged. 40% ABV is on the higher side. Without specific tasting notes, scored as classic Italian-style with mild dark register from barrel aging.

558.name: Forthave Marseille Amaro
558.reasoning: Brooklyn small-batch amaro (medieval-recipe inspired). Eucalyptus + cinnamon + rhubarb + honey. Sweet-honeyed with cooling herbal character; subtle mint from eucalyptus.

175.name: Greenbar Grand Poppy California Amaro
175.reasoning: Organic CA amaro: California poppy, grapefruit, bay leaf, gentian, artichoke. Citrus-forward bitter with the artichoke giving it a root anchor.

618.name: Mödr Amaro
618.reasoning: Utah modern amaro — Wasatch Mountains botanicals. 'Balances herbal complexity, gentle bitterness, refined sweetness.' Oatmeal cookie + earthy botanicals — moderate on most axes.

505.name: Revolution Spirits Amico Amaro
505.reasoning: Texas amaro made 'in the style of Campari or Aperol' — 12 botanicals including hibiscus, sumac, charred cedar, orange peel, gentian, cinchona. Aperitivo-style profile with American botanical twist.

185.name: Riga Black Balsam
185.reasoning: Traditional Latvian bitters (1752). 24 ingredients, 30-day oak aging, 45% ABV — high-intensity. Herbal-forward with dark/earthy register; subtle mint cooling.

634.name: See the Elephant Amaro di Rucola
634.reasoning: Rucola (arugula) amaro from Cilento Coast. Eggnog + sarsaparilla + earthy/peppery. Distinctive vegetal-herbal profile from the arugula maceration.

132.name: Suncliffe Cultivaro
132.reasoning: Arizona's first local amaro — dates, bitter orange, roasted pecans, ponderosa pine bark, nopales. SW take on Italian; balanced herbal-citrus-root with pecan adding nutty body.

635.name: Violet Crown Midnight Marigold Bitter Cordial
635.reasoning: Modern Texas bitter cordial: marigold + turmeric + coriander + caraway. Bright floral nose, honey/brown-sugar sweet, bitter gentian backbone. More cordial than classical amaro — sweet-floral dominant.

538.name: Zucca Rabarbaro
538.reasoning: Italian rhubarb-based amaro. Smoky, earthy, bitter. Lighter and more vegetal than most amari. Smoke character maps to 'dark'; rhubarb root → max 'root'.

596.name: BroVo Amaro #14 by Mike Ryan
596.reasoning: Chocolate-forward amaro: Guatemalan chocolate, thyme, cinnamon, sarsaparilla, angelica, vanilla, strong gentian finish. Agave-sweetened. Dark register dominant; gentian gives root bite.

598.name: BroVo Amaro Kim by Peter Kim
598.reasoning: Chicago regional — bitter-forward (quassia + burdock + gentian) with anar dana, orange peel, milk thistle. Designed for Japanese whiskey / shochu / sake pairing — so dry and bitter, not sweet.

599.name: BroVo Amaro Macquarrie
599.reasoning: Cola-inspired by Miles MacQuarrie of Kimball House (Atlanta tribute). Nutmeg + vanilla + cinnamon + coriander + gentian. Cola flavors cooked into simple syrup. Direct dark-cola family member.

597.name: BroVo Amaro Simo Wallace
597.reasoning: Pouring Ribbons (NYC) — savory, umami-forward. Gentian + Dijon mustard + stewed orchard fruit + thyme + tarragon + baked apple + clove + damp earth + ginger/galangal. Highly distinctive savory profile; very root/earthy with herbal complexity.
